title: Spongotrichum suffruticosum
taxonomy: Spongotrichum suffruticosum is a species of liverwort in the family Lejeuneaceae. It is a type of non-vascular land plant that belongs to the class Marchantiopsida. The genus Spongotrichum is part of the subclass Marchantiineae within the class Marchantiopsida. This liverwort species is likely native to tropical regions, although more specific information on its geographical distribution is needed. Further research is necessary to confirm its exact classification.

intro: Spongotrichum suffruticosum, also known as Spongotrichum, is a type of lichenized fungus that belongs to the family Spongotrichaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing stress, improving skin health, and boosting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Spongotrichum suffruticosum include triterpenoids, such as usnic acid, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial activities. Spongotrichum suffruticosum is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and capsules, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
